checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is the GAS_CAP constant exactly 16777216 (2^24) in all code paths?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Locate all definitions of GAS_CAP or MAX_GAS_LIMIT constants in the codebase. 2. Verify each constant equals exactly 16777216 (0x1000000 or 2^24). 3. Search for hardcoded values that should reference the constant. 4. Submit test transactions with gasLimit at boundary values (16777215, 16777216, 16777217) to verify consistent acceptance/rejection.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-1, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-002, tb-003, tb-005. Network split risk if clients use different cap values.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does txpool reject transactions with gasLimit > 16777216?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit a transaction with gasLimit = 16777217 via RPC. 2. Verify the transaction is rejected with MAX_GAS_LIMIT_EXCEEDED error. 3. Query the mempool to confirm the transaction was not added. 4. Repeat with gasLimit values 16777216 (should accept) and higher values (should reject).
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-2, Trust Boundary: tb-001. First line of defense at transaction entry point.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does P2P-relayed transaction validation match local validation?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Configure a test peer to relay a transaction with gasLimit > 16777216. 2. Verify the receiving node rejects it identically to local submission. 3. Check that no code path bypasses validation based on transaction source. 4. Review code to ensure P2P and local paths converge on same validation logic.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-3, Trust Boundary: tb-002. Malicious peers could attempt to poison mempools.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does block validation reject blocks containing over-cap transactions?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Construct a block containing a transaction with gasLimit = 16777217. 2. Propagate the block via P2P to a test node. 3. Verify the entire block is rejected as invalid. 4. Check that the rejection is logged with appropriate error details. 5. Verify no partial state changes occurred.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-4, Trust Boundary: tb-003. Last line of defense for consensus.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does block validation check ALL transactions without skipping?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Construct blocks with violating transactions at different positions (first, middle, last, only). 2. Verify each block is rejected regardless of violating transaction position. 3. Review validation loop code for early exits, sampling, or short-circuits. 4. Test with maximum transaction count blocks.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-5, Trust Boundaries: tb-003, tb-005. Position-dependent validation bypass risk.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is the entire block rejected when any transaction violates gas cap?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Construct a block with 99 valid transactions and 1 over-cap transaction. 2. Propagate to test node and verify entire block is rejected. 3. Verify no transactions from the block were executed or persisted. 4. Check that partial acceptance mode does not exist in the codebase.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-6, Trust Boundary: tb-003. Partial acceptance causes consensus divergence.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is per-transaction gas cap independent of block gas limit?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Configure a validator with block gas limit > 16777216. 2. Submit a transaction with gasLimit = 16777217. 3. Verify the transaction is rejected despite block limit being higher. 4. Review code to ensure per-tx cap is not derived from block limit. 5. Test both txpool and block validation paths.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-7, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-004, tb-005. Cap confusion could bypass protection.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are both txpool and block validation independently enforcing the gas cap?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify all gas cap check locations in code. 2. Verify at least one check exists in txpool validation. 3. Verify at least one check exists in block validation. 4. Test bypassing txpool (e.g., via Engine API block construction) and verify block validation still catches it. 5. Ensure neither layer assumes the other has validated.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-8, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-002, tb-003, tb-005. Single point of failure risk.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is the gas cap value 16777216 identical at both enforcement points?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Locate the gas cap constant definition(s) in the codebase. 2. Verify txpool references the same constant as block validation. 3. Check for any duplicated or hardcoded cap values that could diverge. 4. Static analysis: ensure both paths use the same constant or identical value. 5. Test transaction with gasLimit in the theoretical discrepancy range if separate constants exist.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-9, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-003. Inconsistent caps cause consensus failure.

checklist.title: Is a rejected transaction dropped and never forwarded to block building?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit a transaction with gasLimit > 16777216 to txpool. 2. Verify the rejection is final and immediate. 3. Monitor internal queues to ensure the transaction never reaches block builder. 4. Review code for any retry or fallback paths that could resurrect rejected transactions. 5. Test edge cases like concurrent submission attempts.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-10, Internal edge: tb-004. Defense-in-depth verification.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is the comparison operator <= (not <) used for gas cap check?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Review code for all gas cap comparison operators. 2. Verify condition is gasLimit <= 16777216 (accept) or gasLimit > 16777216 (reject). 3. Submit transactions with gasLimit = 16777215 (accept), 16777216 (accept), 16777217 (reject). 4. Cross-client test to ensure all clients use the same comparison semantics.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-11, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Off-by-one causes DoS or consensus failure.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Do all clients use identical comparison semantics (> not >=)?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit transaction with gasLimit = 16777216 to each major Ethereum client. 2. Verify all clients accept the transaction identically. 3. Submit gasLimit = 16777217 and verify all clients reject. 4. Document the exact comparison operator used in each client's source code. 5. Alert if any divergence is found.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-12, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-003. Single transaction can split the network.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is RLP encoding of gasLimit strictly validated across all clients?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Craft transactions with non-canonical RLP encoding of gasLimit (leading zeros, oversized integers). 2. Submit to each client and verify uniform rejection. 3. Craft a block with non-canonical gasLimit encoding near the 16777216 boundary. 4. Verify all clients parse identically or uniformly reject. 5. Document any parsing differences.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-13, Trust Boundary: tb-003. Encoding ambiguity causes consensus divergence.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is per-tx cap enforced even when block gas limit exceeds it?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Create a block with header gas limit > 16777216 containing an over-cap transaction. 2. Propagate to test nodes and verify rejection. 3. Review code to confirm per-tx cap is checked independently of block.gasLimit. 4. Test with validator configured to set high block gas limits.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-14, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Per-tx cap must not be confused with block limit.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is gas cap validation O(1) per transaction with minimal memory?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Review validation code to confirm it's a simple integer comparison. 2. Benchmark block validation with maximum transaction count. 3. Verify no per-transaction memory allocations occur during gas cap check. 4. Measure CPU time and memory for worst-case blocks. 5. Verify validation time is proportional to transaction count.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-15, Trust Boundary: tb-003. Inefficient validation enables timing attacks.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does block validation catch over-cap transactions from direct block construction?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Construct a block directly via Engine API (bypassing txpool). 2. Include a transaction with gasLimit > 16777216. 3. Verify block validation rejects the block. 4. Review code to ensure block validation does not trust that txpool already validated. 5. Test with MEV block builders in the validation chain.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-16, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Block builders could bypass txpool.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does transaction replacement re-validate gasLimit?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit a valid transaction with gasLimit = 16777216. 2. Submit a replacement (same nonce, higher fee) with gasLimit = 16777217. 3. Verify the replacement is rejected. 4. Review transaction replacement code to ensure all fields are revalidated. 5. Test various replacement scenarios (EIP-1559 fee bumps, etc.).
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-17, Trust Boundary: tb-001. TOCTOU in replacement could bypass validation.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is gasLimit covered by the transaction signature?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Create a signed transaction with gasLimit = 16777216. 2. Modify the gasLimit field after signing (set to 16777217). 3. Submit to a node and verify signature verification fails. 4. Review transaction signing/verification code to confirm gasLimit is in the signed payload. 5. Test for all transaction types (legacy, EIP-1559, EIP-4844).
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-18, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-002. P2P tampering must be detectable.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are peers penalized for sending over-cap transactions?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Configure a test peer to repeatedly send transactions with gasLimit > 16777216. 2. Monitor peer score changes on the receiving node. 3. Verify the peer is eventually disconnected after threshold violations. 4. Test that legitimate peers are not penalized for occasional invalid transactions. 5. Review peer scoring configuration for appropriate thresholds.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-19, Trust Boundaries: tb-002, tb-009. Peer flooding protection.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ModExp multiplication_complexity >= 16 for all inputs?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with various base_length and modulus_length combinations. 2. Verify complexity is 16 for max_length <= 32 and >= 50 for max_length > 32. 3. Test edge cases: max_length = 0, 1, 32, 33. 4. Search for any input that produces complexity < 16. 5. Compare gas charged across all major clients.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-20, Trust Boundaries: tb-007, tb-008. Complexity floor prevents DoS.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is complexity exactly 16 when max_length <= 32?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with max(base_length, modulus_length) = 32. 2. Verify multiplication_complexity = 16. 3. Call with max_length = 33 and verify complexity = 50. 4. Test the exact boundary condition across all clients. 5. Document any deviations from expected values.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-21, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Boundary inconsistency causes consensus failure.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is complexity formula correct for max_length > 32 with overflow protection?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify formula: complexity = 2 * ceil(max_length/8)^2 for max_length > 32. 2. Test with large base_length/modulus_length values near integer limits. 3. Verify no integer overflow in words^2 computation. 4. Use static analysis to check for overflow-safe arithmetic. 5. Test with inputs designed to trigger overflow.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-22, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Overflow wraps gas cost enabling DoS.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is iteration_count exactly 1 when exponent is 0?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with exponent = 0 and exponent_length <= 32. 2. Verify iteration_count = 1 (not 0). 3. Verify gas cost is max(500, complexity * 1) = 500 for small inputs. 4. Test across all major Ethereum clients. 5. Check for division-by-zero or other edge case issues.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-23, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Zero exponent edge case.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is iteration_count = bit_length(exponent) - 1 for non-zero exponent <= 32 bytes?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with various exponent values (1, 2, 255, 256, 2^32-1). 2. Verify iteration_count = bit_length(exponent) - 1 for each. 3. Test power-of-2 boundaries where bit_length changes. 4. Compare results across all major clients. 5. Document any bit_length calculation differences.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-24, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Cross-client consistency required.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is large exponent multiplier 16 (not 8 from EIP-2565)?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with exponent_length = 64 (> 32). 2. Calculate expected gas: 16 * (64-32) + bit_length(top_32_bytes). 3. Verify actual gas matches (multiplier 16, not 8). 4. Compare gas across all clients for the same inputs. 5. Alert if any client uses multiplier 8.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-25, Trust Boundary: tb-007. EIP-7883 uses 16, not EIP-2565's 8.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is 16*(exponent_length-32) overflow-protected?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Calculate max safe exponent_length: uint64_max / 16 + 32. 2. Call ModExp with exponent_length near overflow boundary. 3. Verify no integer overflow occurs (gas remains very large, not wrapped to small). 4. Review code for overflow-checked arithmetic. 5. Test with various integer type implementations.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-26, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Overflow enables free expensive operations.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is final gas = max(500, complexity * iterations) without /3?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with known inputs and calculate expected gas. 2. Verify formula: max(500, floor(complexity * iterations)). 3. Explicitly verify NO division by 3 is applied. 4. Compare gas across all clients. 5. Alert if any client's gas is 3x lower than expected (indicating /3 still present).
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-27, Trust Boundary: tb-008. EIP-7883 removes the /3 divisor.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is minimum gas floor 500 (not 200)?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with minimal inputs producing complexity * iterations < 500. 2. Verify exactly 500 gas is charged. 3. Verify NOT 200 gas (old EIP-2565 minimum). 4. Test across all clients. 5. Calculate maximum calls per transaction at new vs old minimum.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-28, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Higher minimum limits attack amplification.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ModExp gas cost deterministic and identical across all clients?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Create a comprehensive test vector suite covering all formula paths. 2. Execute identical ModExp calls on all major clients. 3. Compare gas consumed for each test case. 4. Alert on any divergence. 5. Include edge cases, boundary values, and large inputs.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-29, Trust Boundary: tb-008. Gas divergence breaks consensus.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is all ModExp arithmetic overflow-protected (256-bit or arbitrary precision)?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Review ModExp gas calculation code for integer types used. 2. Verify ceil, words^2, 2*words^2, 16*(exp_len-32) use safe arithmetic. 3. Test with inputs near uint64 and uint256 boundaries. 4. Use fuzzing to find overflow-inducing inputs. 5. Document precision requirements for each intermediate value.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-30, Trust Boundaries: tb-007, tb-008. Comprehensive overflow protection required.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is complexity * iterations product overflow-protected?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Find input combinations where both complexity and iterations are large. 2. Calculate theoretical product and verify no overflow. 3. Test with base_length near max and exponent_length near max. 4. Verify gas remains very large (exceeds transaction limit) for such inputs. 5. Review code for checked multiplication.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-31, Trust Boundaries: tb-007, tb-008. Product overflow enables free execution.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is memory allocation bounded before EIP-7823 limits applied?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with very large base_length or modulus_length values. 2. Monitor memory allocation before gas check. 3. Verify memory limits are enforced before allocation. 4. Test with base_length = 2^32 and insufficient gas. 5. Verify no OOM or crash occurs.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-32, Trust Boundaries: tb-007, tb-008. OOM can crash validator nodes.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is max_length 32/33 branch boundary identical across clients?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with max_length = 32 on all clients; verify complexity = 16. 2. Call with max_length = 33 on all clients; verify complexity = 50. 3. Document exact branch condition in each client's code. 4. Alert on any <= vs < confusion at boundary. 5. Test both base_length = 32 and modulus_length = 32 variations.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-33, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Branch inconsistency causes consensus failure.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is there NO division by 3 in the EIP-7883 formula?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Review ModExp gas calculation code for any /3 or div(3) operations. 2. Call ModExp and compare actual gas with formula with and without /3. 3. Verify actual gas matches complexity * iterations (not complexity * iterations / 3). 4. Cross-client comparison to detect /3 presence. 5. Alert if any client's gas is exactly 1/3 of expected.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-34, Trust Boundary: tb-008. Most critical EIP-7883 change.

checklist.title: Is the minimum 500 gas floor always applied when computed gas < 500?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Find all input combinations where complexity * iterations < 500. 2. For each, verify exactly 500 gas is charged. 3. Test small operands: base_length=1, mod_length=1, exp_length=1, exp=0. 4. Verify no path bypasses the max(500, ...) check. 5. Document any inputs that produce < 500 gas.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-35, Trust Boundaries: tb-007, tb-008.

checklist.title: Is ModExp execution time bounded and proportional to gas charged?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Benchmark ModExp with various inputs measuring CPU time vs gas charged. 2. Plot time/gas ratio across input space. 3. Identify any outliers with disproportionately high time/gas ratio. 4. Test worst-case inputs at maximum allowed gas. 5. Verify total block processing time remains acceptable.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-36, Trust Boundaries: tb-007, tb-008.

checklist.title: Is block processing time acceptable with maximum ModExp calls at minimum gas?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Deploy a contract making repeated minimum-gas ModExp calls. 2. Execute transaction with gasLimit = 16777216. 3. Measure total execution time for ~33,554 ModExp calls. 4. Verify execution completes within acceptable attestation window. 5. Test on representative validator hardware.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-37, Trust Boundary: tb-007.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does ModExp check gas availability BEFORE executing?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with insufficient gas for the operation. 2. Verify the call reverts immediately without executing the computation. 3. Monitor CPU usage to confirm no significant computation occurred. 4. Review code to verify gas check order: parse -> compute cost -> check -> allocate -> execute. 5. Test with just-below-sufficient gas.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-38, Trust Boundary: tb-008.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is iteration_count >= 1 for all inputs?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Test inputs designed to minimize iteration_count. 2. Verify max(iteration_count, 1) is applied. 3. Check large exponent path: 16*(exp_len-32) + bit_length(top_32) >= 1. 4. Test with top_32_bytes = 0 to minimize bit_length contribution. 5. Verify no zero-cost execution path exists.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-39, Trust Boundaries: tb-007, tb-008.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does ModExp handle all edge case inputs deterministically?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Test edge cases: base_length=0, modulus_length=0, exponent_length=0. 2. Test all-zero inputs and single-byte inputs. 3. Test max_length at 32/33 boundary. 4. Test very large length values. 5. Execute on all major clients and compare gas and results.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-44, Trust Boundary: tb-007.

checklist.title: Is gas = max(500, 16*1) = 500 for smallest ModExp inputs?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with max_length <= 32 and exponent = 0. 2. Verify: complexity = 16, iterations = 1, gas = max(500, 16) = 500. 3. Test across all clients. 4. This is the absolute minimum gas for any ModExp call. 5. Document any deviation.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-45, Trust Boundaries: tb-007, tb-008.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are P2P messages always independently validated regardless of peer reputation?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Establish a peer with good reputation through normal behavior. 2. Switch the peer to sending over-cap transactions. 3. Verify each transaction is still validated and rejected. 4. Review code for any cached validation shortcuts based on peer. 5. Test that no 'trusted peer' bypass exists.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-46, Trust Boundaries: tb-002, tb-009.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ModExp gas computed before memory allocation?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with large lengths and minimal gas. 2. Monitor memory allocation during the call. 3. Verify NO memory is allocated before gas check fails. 4. Review code order: parse lengths -> compute gas -> check gas -> allocate. 5. Test with lengths designed to trigger OOM if allocation came first.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-47, Trust Boundary: tb-008.

checklist.title: Do specific EIP-7883 test vectors produce expected gas costs?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Test vector 1: base=1, mod=1, exp=1, exp_val=2 -> gas=500. 2. Test vector 2: base=64, mod=64, exp=64, bit_length=256 -> gas=98176. 3. Execute on all clients and compare. 4. Document exact gas consumed for each. 5. Alert on any deviation from expected values.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-48, Trust Boundaries: tb-007, tb-008.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is EIP-7825 gas cap activated only at Fusaka fork block?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit gasLimit > 16777216 transaction at fork block - 1. 2. Verify it is accepted (pre-fork behavior). 3. Submit same transaction at fork block. 4. Verify it is rejected (post-fork behavior). 5. Compare fork activation across all clients.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-49, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-003.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is EIP-7883 ModExp formula activated only at Fusaka fork block?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Execute ModExp at fork block - 1 and record gas (EIP-2565 formula). 2. Execute identical ModExp at fork block and record gas (EIP-7883 formula). 3. Verify gas difference matches formula change (removal of /3, minimum 500). 4. Compare across all clients. 5. Alert on any activation timing mismatch.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B12-50, Trust Boundary: tb-008.
